name: SDLC Delivery Team
description: Eight pre-wired agents that cover software delivery end to end on one codebase: a solution architect scopes the work into a story-pointed plan, a dev clone implements that plan (writes the code + tests, runs them) and scans for backlog, a QA guardian tests each PR the dev clone opens in an isolated git worktree and then merges it (auto-merge per the project's standards policy) or returns it for fixes, a code reviewer guards quality, a security sentinel audits for vulnerabilities, a release manager versions and ships, a docs steward keeps the README and changelog in sync, and an artist generates on-brand UI/visual assets for the feature in parallel with the build. They hand work forward through events and share the same codebase and memory. (The artist needs an image-generation credential, and QA Guardian's PR-merge action needs a GitHub credential; without those they stay idle and the build cascade is unaffected.)

team.name: SDLC Delivery Team
team.description: Architect → implement → QA-test/merge → review → security → release → docs — end-to-end software lifecycle on one codebase.

group.name: Software Delivery
group.shared_instructions: Every member operates on the same registered codebase. Ground every assertion in codebase evidence — cite file:line rather than speculating. Hand work forward through events, not direct calls. Track decisions, findings, and version history in memory so the team's knowledge compounds across runs instead of resetting each time. Prefer empirical findings over assumptions; when a precondition (codebase access) is missing, say so explicitly rather than fabricating results. SHARED KNOWLEDGE GRAPH (Obsidian): the team shares an Obsidian vault knowledge graph. MCP tools are deferred, so FIRST call ToolSearch with `select:obsidian_vault_search,obsidian_vault_write_note` to load their schemas. BEFORE changing code or deciding, obsidian_vault_search for prior decisions/constraints touching the files/modules you will change and honor them (do not re-litigate settled decisions). AFTER a significant decision, write a durable decision-note via obsidian_vault_write_note (title "Decision: <short-slug>") that links related modules/constraints with [[wikilinks]] (e.g. [[Module: rankings]], [[Constraint: test-harness]]) so the graph compounds across runs.

connections.label: scoped, story-pointed task breakdown to implement (architecture.analysis.completed)
opened PR to test in an isolated worktree + merge or return (dev-clone.pr.created)
tested + merged PR ready to ship (qa.pr.approved)
tests failed — changes requested back to the author (qa.pr.changes_requested)
shipped + documented state for the next review cycle (docs.sync.completed)
scoped feature for UI/brand visual assets, in parallel with the build (architecture.analysis.completed)
